behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection plus CLI parsing of actual `git` output. A unit test can validate categorization logic, but it will miss real subprocess/flag behavior (for example, rename detection not enabled) that turns renames into add+delete. Higher levels are disqualified because behavior text contains no AI/ML or multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against that repo, and assert report shows exactly one rename and zero corresponding add/delete for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly depends on real git diff/subprocess behavior with binary content and real stdout/stderr decoding paths. Mocked diff text can hide encoding and subprocess edge failures. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no model output quality or agent pipeline involved.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, commit and then modify/add a binary file, run CLI, assert process exits successfully and binary file contributes to counts in report.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure appears at real git subprocess boundary (`HEAD` missing / unborn branch behavior). In-process fakes can simulate errors but may miss actual exit codes/messages and CLI handling. Agentic/Workflow are rejected because behavior is deterministic CLI-git interaction only.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert zero exit failure and an empty structured report on stdout.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against real repository history semantics, requiring real git state transitions and subprocess interactions. A fake history feed can verify counting logic but cannot validate that the CLI extracts true history correctly from git. No AI/ML or multi-agent sequence is present.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run CLI, and assert each reported category count equals the known ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real git index vs working tree semantics, which are OS/subprocess-backed and not faithfully reproduced by simple mocks. Integration is too low because the critical failure mode is at git state boundary. Higher levels are disqualified: no LLM decision or multi-step agent chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create identical file changes in two scenarios (staged vs unstaged), run CLI in each scenario, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured output.
